svg.text: ━ CASCADE ╌ ╌ SET NULL 🔑 PK ● FK (target table icon) [U] Unique [I] Indexed RLS = Row-Level Security
Operational tables record every artefact produced during a OpenScientist analysis run — uploaded data, dialogue, billing, step-by-step logs, iteration metrics, user feedback and generated plots.
An uploaded crystallography data file (MTZ reflection data, CIF coordinates or PDB model). Tracks the on-disk path, MIME type and byte size.
A single message in the user–assistant dialogue within a job. Messages are stored in order and distinguished by role (user / assistant) to reconstruct the conversation.
Per-call LLM billing record capturing provider, model, input / output token counts and calculated cost in USD. Powers the Vertex AI budget enforcer's real-time spend tracking.
An individual step in the AI agent's workflow — reasoning, code execution or literature search — with its input, output, timing and success flag. Provides a full audit trail of every action
Roll-up of a single analysis iteration: narrative summary, key findings, hypothesis counts, code-execution count and quantitative metrics such as R-factors and completeness.
A user correction, suggestion or approval provided during the analysis. The applied flag tracks whether the AI agent incorporated the feedback in subsequent iterations.
A visualisation generated by the AI agent (histogram, scatter plot, heatmap etc.). Stores the image path, descriptive metadata and the Python code used to regenerate the plot.
Every operational record inherits access control from its parent job. The job owner has full read-write access. View-level share holders can read data files, messages, logs and plots but cannot
modify them. Edit-level share holders can create and update records as collaborators.
